args.query: flight search task completed successfully but user command ended with send message [EXIT] after reaching results; check for premature termination / early_terminated pattern

items.summary: The agent exited before reaching a page state that satisfies the task.
items.fix_hint: Require a final evidence check that the requested information or state is visible before sending an exit message.

items.summary: The agent completed a search task but did not enforce an explicit user constraint before stopping.
items.fix_hint: Track hard constraints separately from the main goal and verify them against the final page state before returning success.

items.summary: The agent used broad browsing steps instead of applying the available search or filter controls.
items.fix_hint: Prefer task-specific search inputs and filters when the target can be narrowed directly.

args.query: Google Flights task: one-way Sydney to San Francisco on March 10, 2026, shortest layover; ended on search results or early exit

args.task: navigate: Search for one-way flights from Sydney to San Francisco, departing on March 10, 2026, with the shortest layover.

result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Google Flights results page for one-way Sydney → San Francisco on Tue, Mar 10. “Best” tab selected; top flights list visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
“One way”, “Sydney”, “San Francisco”, “Tue, Mar 10”, “Top flights”, “Sorted by top flights”, “1 stop”, “Nonstop”, “18 hr 20 min”, “13 hr 30 min”, “13 hr 45 min”, prices “$620”, “$756”, “$757”.
selected_candidate:
Air New Zealand option: 11:45 AM – 12:05 PM, 1 stop, 18 hr 20 min, $620.
constraint_evidence:
- one-way flight: supported (“One way” visible)
- origin Sydney: supported (“Sydney” visible)
- destination San Francisco: supported (“San Francisco” visible)
- departing on March 10, 2026: supported by URL context and visible date “Tue, Mar 10”
- shortest layover: not_visible (no layover duration shown for any option; only stop count and total duration visible)
- flight results available: supported (top flights list visible)
- must be a flight option from Sydney to San Francisco: supported (all visible results show SYD–SFO)
action_target:
Send exit message to user: “[EXIT]”
success_signals:
User-facing exit message sent; no further browser interaction.
failure_signals:
Any navigation/click instead of exiting; claiming shortest layover selection without layover data; missing the exit message.
uncertainty:
The screenshot does not expose layover durations, so the “shortest layover” criterion cannot be verified from visible evidence.

task_context.task: navigate: Search for one-way flights from Sydney to San Francisco, departing on March 10, 2026, with the shortest layover.
task_context.title: Find Cheap Flights Worldwide & Book Your Ticket - Google Flights

observation.title: Find Cheap Flights Worldwide & Book Your Ticket - Google Flights

evidence.claim: The run reached Google Flights results for one-way Sydney → San Francisco on Tue, Mar 10, 2026.
The final step sent the exit message '[EXIT]', indicating the agent stopped after presenting results.

Visible results include flight options and durations/prices, but layover durations are not visible in the screenshot, so the 'shortest layover' constraint cannot be verified from the page state alone.
A failure-memory precedent matches this pattern: exiting before fully verifying a task-specific constraint.
A related precedent warns about completing the main search goal without enforcing an explicit constraint before stopping.

args.expected_behavior: After reaching search results, verify the flight option with the shortest layover and only then exit; if layover durations are not visible, continue refining or inspecting results until the constraint is satisfied.
args.actual_behavior: The agent ended the run at the search results page and sent '[EXIT]' without visible evidence that the shortest-layover constraint was verified.
args.regression_rule: Before terminating a flight-search task, confirm the final page visibly supports every explicit constraint in the prompt; if a constraint such as 'shortest layover' is not visible, do not exit yet.

suggested_followups.label: Inspect step 17
suggested_followups.message: Open the high-detail evidence for the final results page and check whether layover duration is visible anywhere.

suggested_followups.label: Compare with a success
suggested_followups.message: Find a successful similar run and compare how it verified the shortest-layover constraint before exiting.
